pois.name: Speyerer Dom
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site and the largest preserved Romanesque church in the world, serving as the burial place of eight Holy Roman Emperors and kings.

pois.name: Porta Nigra
pois.description: The best-preserved Roman city gate north of the Alps, this massive sandstone structure dates back to the 2nd century and is a primary landmark of Trier.

pois.name: Burg Eltz
pois.description: A magnificent medieval castle tucked away in a side valley of the Moselle, which has remained in the possession of the same family for over 800 years.

pois.name: Mainzer Dom St. Martin
pois.description: A masterpiece of Romanesque architecture that dominates the skyline of Mainz, featuring an impressive interior with numerous tombs of former Archbishops.

pois.name: Wormser Dom St. Peter
pois.description: One of the three great imperial cathedrals of the Rhine, this Romanesque structure was the site of the famous Diet of Worms in 1521.

pois.name: Abtei Maria Laach
pois.description: A high Romanesque Benedictine monastery located on the shores of a volcanic lake, known for its remarkably preserved architecture and active monastic life.

pois.name: Marksburg
pois.description: The only hilltop castle on the Middle Rhine that was never destroyed, offering an authentic glimpse into medieval defensive architecture and daily life.

pois.name: Hambacher Schloss
pois.description: Known as the cradle of German democracy due to the Hambach Festival of 1832, where thousands gathered to demand national unity and liberal rights.

pois.name: Reichsburg Cochem
pois.description: A prominent neo-Gothic castle perched on a hill above the Moselle river, rebuilt in the 19th century on the ruins of a much older imperial fortress.

pois.name: Deutsches Eck
pois.description: The point where the Moselle flows into the Rhine in Koblenz, marked by a monumental equestrian statue of Emperor William I.

pois.name: Burg Trifels
pois.description: A former imperial castle where the English King Richard the Lionheart was once held prisoner; it also once housed the Imperial Regalia.

pois.name: Burg Rheinfels
pois.description: Once the strongest fortress on the Rhine, this massive ruin above Sankt Goar features a complex system of underground mines and tunnels.

pois.name: Kaiserthermen
pois.description: The remains of large Roman imperial baths in Trier, featuring impressive brickwork walls and a network of subterranean service tunnels.

pois.name: Burg Pfalzgrafenstein
pois.description: A unique ship-shaped toll castle situated on a small island in the middle of the Rhine, built to exact duties from passing merchant vessels.

pois.name: Schloss Stolzenfels
pois.description: The epitome of Rhine Romanticism, this palace was built by the Prussian King Frederick William IV on the ruins of a medieval castle.

pois.name: Dreimühlen-Wasserfall
pois.description: A unique waterfall in the Eifel created by calcium carbonate deposits that cause the rock to grow continuously by several centimeters each year.

pois.name: Teufelstisch
pois.description: A bizarre 14-meter-high bunter sandstone formation resembling a table, located in the Palatinate Forest near Hinterweidenthal.

pois.name: Loreley
pois.description: A steep slate rock overlooking the narrowest part of the Rhine, famous for its legend of the siren who lured sailors to their doom.

pois.name: Laacher See
pois.description: A caldera lake formed by a massive volcanic eruption approximately 13,000 years ago, still showing signs of volcanic activity through mofettes.

pois.name: Geysir Andernach
pois.description: The world's highest cold-water geyser, powered by volcanic carbon dioxide rather than steam, erupting periodically on the Namedyer Werth island.

pois.name: Römerbrücke
pois.description: The oldest bridge in Germany, this Roman structure in Trier still carries vehicle traffic over the Moselle on its original 2nd-century stone pillars.

pois.name: Heiliger Sand
pois.description: The oldest Jewish cemetery in Europe, located in Worms, with approximately 2,500 gravestones dating back as far as the 11th century.

pois.name: St. Stephan zu Mainz
pois.description: A Gothic church internationally renowned for its luminous blue stained-glass windows designed by the Jewish artist Marc Chagall.

pois.name: Kloster Himmerod
pois.description: A Cistercian abbey founded by Saint Bernard of Clairvaux, featuring a Baroque church in the secluded Salm river valley of the Eifel.

pois.name: Burg Thurant
pois.description: A twin-towered castle overlooking the Moselle, once jointly owned by the Archbishops of Cologne and Trier after a long siege.

pois.name: Bacharach Altstadt
pois.description: One of the most picturesque medieval towns in the Rhine Valley, famous for its timber-framed houses and the Gothic Wernerkapelle ruin.

pois.name: Bernkastel-Kues Marktplatz
pois.description: A world-famous market square in the Moselle region featuring remarkably narrow half-timbered houses and a Renaissance town hall.

pois.name: Burg Sooneck
pois.description: Originally a robber knight's lair, this castle was rebuilt in the 19th century as a hunting lodge for the Hohenzollern princes.

pois.name: Burg Katz
pois.description: Built by the Counts of Katzenelnbogen, this castle provides a striking silhouette above Sankt Goarshausen and the Rhine.

pois.name: Festung Ehrenbreitstein
pois.description: One of the largest preserved fortifications in Europe, this Prussian fortress offers panoramic views over the confluence of the Rhine and Moselle.

pois.name: Hardenburg
pois.description: A massive ruin of a Renaissance palace and fortress near Bad Dürkheim, showcasing the power of the Counts of Leiningen.

pois.name: Burg Nanstein
pois.description: The site of the famous 1523 siege and death of Franz von Sickingen, the 'last knight', during the Knights' Revolt.

pois.name: Meisenheim Altstadt
pois.description: A remarkably well-preserved historic center on the Glan river that escaped destruction in wars, featuring fine late-Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Freinsheimer Stadtmauer
pois.description: A perfectly preserved medieval city wall nearly one kilometer long, encircling the historic core of this famous wine-growing town.

pois.name: Pulvermaar
pois.description: A near-perfectly circular volcanic maar lake in the Vulkaneifel, known for its deep blue water and status as one of Germany's deepest lakes.

pois.name: Erbeskopf
pois.description: The highest peak in Rhineland-Palatinate, located in the Hunsrück-Hochwald National Park, featuring the 'Windklang' sculpture and observation platform.

pois.name: Altes Haus
pois.description: A famous medieval timber-framed house in Bacharach that has served as an inn for centuries, surviving numerous floods and wars.

pois.name: Brauhaus zum Kloster Machern
pois.description: A historic brewery and monastery site on the banks of the Moselle, now hosting a museum and traditional dining facilities.

pois.name: Burg Berwartstein
pois.description: A rock-cut castle in the Palatinate Forest famous for its legendary owner, the robber knight Hans Trapp.

pois.name: Dahner Felsenburg-Gruppe
pois.description: A triple complex of ruins (Altdahn, Grafendahn, and Tanstein) carved into massive sandstone rocks above the town of Dahn.

pois.name: Schloss Villa Ludwigshöhe
pois.description: A summer residence built by King Ludwig I of Bavaria in the Italian villa style, situated on the edge of the Palatinate Forest.

pois.name: Herrstein Altstadt
pois.description: A charming 'Fachwerk' village on the German Gemstone Route, featuring over 60 restored half-timbered buildings and a clock tower.

pois.name: Monreal Altstadt
pois.description: Known as the pearl of the Eifel, this village features red-and-white half-timbered houses beneath the ruins of the Löwenburg and Philippsburg.

pois.name: Schloss Engers
pois.description: A late Baroque hunting palace on the Rhine, renowned for its ornate Hall of Diana and its current role as a center for classical music.

pois.name: Genovevaburg
pois.description: A late medieval castle in Mayen that houses the Eifel Museum and the German Slate Mine Museum in its subterranean caverns.

pois.name: Burg Lissingen
pois.description: One of the few castles in the Eifel never to have been destroyed, this moated fortress is divided into an Upper and Lower castle.

pois.name: Wildenburg
pois.description: High-altitude ruins in the Hunsrück mountains, offering views over the Idar Forest and featuring a reconstructed gatehouse.

pois.name: Burg Arras
pois.description: The only fortress in the Alf valley, which once served as an imperial defensive outpost for the Palatinate and Moselle regions.

pois.name: Drachenfels (Pfalz)
pois.description: A rock-cut fortress in the Wasgau region with extensive chambers and stairs hewn directly into the sandstone plateau.

pois.name: Konstantinbasilika
pois.description: An enormous Roman brick structure in Trier that served as Emperor Constantine's throne room; it is the largest single-room Roman building still standing.

pois.name: Liebfrauenkirche Trier
pois.description: Adjacent to the Trier Cathedral, this is the oldest Gothic church in Germany, built on a unique circular floor plan.

pois.name: Römerturm Dill
pois.description: A small Roman tower ruin in the Hunsrück region that highlights the importance of inland communication routes during the empire.

pois.name: Schloss Montabaur
pois.description: A prominent yellow-walled palace and former electoral residence that sits on a hill overlooking the Westerwald town of Montabaur.

pois.name: Abtei Sayn
pois.description: A Premonstratensian monastery known for its Romanesque cloister and unique colorful medieval wall paintings in the church.

pois.name: Kloster Springiersbach
pois.description: A Carmelite monastery in the Kondelwald forest featuring an impressive Baroque church with notable stucco work.

pois.name: Wernerkapelle Bacharach
pois.description: The skeletal Gothic ruin of a pilgrimage chapel, standing as a monument of intricate stonework above the Rhine town of Bacharach.

pois.name: Michaelskapelle Koblenz
pois.description: A late medieval chapel situated near the Moselle banks, often passed by cyclists and walkers on the river promenade.

pois.name: Elwedritsche-Brunnen
pois.description: A fountain in Neustadt an der Weinstraße dedicated to the mythical forest creatures of Palatinate folklore known as Elwedritsche.

pois.name: Burg Olbrück
pois.description: A landmark ruin in the Brohl Valley of the Eifel, featuring a climbable 34-meter high keep with views of the surrounding volcanic peaks.

pois.name: Bitburger Brauerei
pois.description: The main facility of one of Germany's most famous pilsner producers, offering insight into industrial brewing history in the town of Bitburg.
